phrases.intent_en: Comment on the beautiful horse statues
phrases.text_local: Ces statues de chevaux sont magnifiques ! Elles sont tellement réalistes.
phrases.pronunciation: say stah-TOO duh shuh-VOH sohn mah-nyee-FEEK ! el sohn tel-MAHN ray-ah-LEEST.
phrases.cultural_note: Bronze equestrian statues ('les statues équestres') are a proud tradition across Europe and the Americas. Lexington's life-sized racing statues are genuinely striking — this is an authentic reaction.

phrases.intent_en: Ask someone to take a photo of you
phrases.text_local: Excusez-moi, pourriez-vous prendre une photo de moi devant les statues ?
phrases.pronunciation: ex-KYOO-zay mwah, poor-YAY voo PRAHN-druh oon FOH-toh duh mwah duh-VAHN lay stah-TOO ?
phrases.cultural_note: The French take photos ('des photos') very seriously — composition, framing, and light all matter. Asking nicely and showing the shot afterward to say 'merci, elle est parfaite !' is good etiquette.

responses.french: Je prends plusieurs photos pour vous assurer un bon résultat.
responses.english: I'll take a few photos to make sure you get a good one.
responses.phonetic: zhuh prahn ploo-ZYUR FOH-toh poor voo ah-soo-RAY un bohn ray-zool-TAH.

phrases.intent_en: Ask a passerby about the history of Lexington's horse culture
phrases.text_local: Pardon, pouvez-vous me parler un peu de la culture équestre de Lexington ?
phrases.pronunciation: par-DOHN, poo-VAY voo muh par-LAY un puh duh lah kool-TOOR ay-KEST-ruh duh LEX-ing-tun ?
phrases.cultural_note: Lexingtonians are immensely proud of their horse heritage. Asking about it is an instant conversation starter — locals love to share stories about Bluegrass Country and its famous farms.

responses.french: Avec plaisir ! Lexington est la capitale mondiale de l'élevage de chevaux de course.
responses.english: With pleasure! Lexington is the world capital of thoroughbred horse breeding.
responses.phonetic: ah-VEK play-ZEER ! LEX-ing-tun ay lah kah-pee-TAL mohn-DYAL duh lay-luh-VAZH duh shuh-VOH duh KOORS.

responses.french: La région du Bluegrass est idéale pour élever des chevaux — l'herbe est riche en minéraux.
responses.english: The Bluegrass region is ideal for raising horses — the grass is rich in minerals.
responses.phonetic: lah ray-ZHYON doo BLOO-grass ay ee-day-AL poor ay-luh-VAY day shuh-VOH — lair-buh ay REESH ahn mee-nay-ROH.

phrases.intent_en: Tell someone you're visiting from France
phrases.text_local: Je viens de France — c'est ma première visite à Lexington. La ville me plaît beaucoup !
phrases.pronunciation: zhuh VYAN duh FRAHNS — say mah pruh-MYAIR vee-ZEET ah LEX-ing-tun. lah VEEL muh PLAY bo-KOO !
phrases.cultural_note: Saying you're from France ('Je viens de France') will often spark genuine enthusiasm from Lexington locals — especially around horses, as Normandy is famous for its own thoroughbred industry and connections to Kentucky.

The statues at Thoroughbred Park depict racehorses in full gallop, celebrating Lexington's identity as the 'Horse Capital of the World.' Asking about public art ('l'art public') is a very French thing to do.

responses.french: Ces statues représentent des chevaux de course au galop — c'est un hommage à l'héritage équestre de Lexington.
responses.english: These statues depict racehorses at full gallop — they're a tribute to Lexington's equestrian heritage.
responses.phonetic: say stah-TOO ruh-pray-ZAHNT day shuh-VOH duh KOORS oh gah-LOH — say tun oh-MAZH ah lay-ray-TAZH ay-KEST-ruh duh LEX-ing-tun.
